assumptions: The analysis is performed in the consolidated financial statements, because the receivable arises within the consolidation perimeter.
The declared dividend has created an intragroup monetary receivable denominated in a foreign currency, so the IFRS 9 guidance on intragroup foreign-currency hedged items is potentially relevant.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies fair value hedge accounting for a recognised asset or liability exposed to a particular risk affecting profit or loss, making it a credible model to assess for a recognised intragroup foreign-currency receivable.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 separately recognises cash flow hedge accounting for variability in cash flows of recognised items or highly probable forecast transactions, so it remains a distinct hedge accounting model worth testing for the foreign-currency exposure.
